question: I have morning stiffness in my hands that lasts over an hour and my joints are swollen. Could this be rheumatoid arthritis and how is it diagnosed?
I was told I have a positive ANA test. My primary care doctor referred me to a rheumatologist. Does a positive ANA mean I have lupus?
I have gout and my uric acid is consistently high despite diet changes. My rheumatologist wants to start allopurinol. When is this medication necessary?
I have lupus and was just told my kidneys are affected. What is lupus nephritis and how serious is it?
I have rheumatoid arthritis and my doctor prescribed methotrexate. I'm worried about side effects. What monitoring is required?
I have psoriatic arthritis affecting my fingers and spine. My rheumatologist wants to start a biologic. What biologics are used and how do they work?
I have ankylosing spondylitis with severe back pain and stiffness. My doctor mentioned TNF inhibitors. What are my medication options?
I was diagnosed with Sjogren's syndrome. I have dry eyes and dry mouth. What other organ systems can Sjogren's affect?
I have widespread muscle pain and fatigue and my rheumatologist says I have fibromyalgia. This isn't an inflammatory condition — why did I see a rheumatologist?
I have a new severe headache, jaw pain when chewing, and scalp tenderness. I'm 68. My rheumatologist is very concerned. What could this be?
I have scleroderma and my fingers turn white and blue in the cold. I also have difficulty swallowing. What are the complications of systemic sclerosis?
I have osteoporosis from long-term steroid use. My DEXA scan shows a T-score of -2.8. What medications are available beyond calcium and vitamin D?
My rheumatoid arthritis is in remission on a biologic. My rheumatologist wants to taper the medication. What is the risk of flaring during taper?
I have pseudogout in my knee and it keeps flaring. What causes pseudogout and how is it different from gout?
I have inflammatory myositis and elevated muscle enzymes. My rheumatologist is deciding between dermatomyositis and polymyositis. What is the difference?
I need knee replacement surgery but I'm on methotrexate for RA. My surgeon says I need to stop it before surgery. How does this affect my RA management?
I was prescribed hydroxychloroquine for lupus. My ophthalmologist wants annual eye checks. Why does this drug require eye monitoring?
I have vasculitis involving my kidneys and lungs. My rheumatologist said my ANCA test is positive. What is ANCA-associated vasculitis and how urgent is treatment?
I have reactive arthritis after a gastrointestinal infection. Will this go away on its own or become chronic?
I was recently started on a JAK inhibitor for RA. My rheumatologist mentioned some cardiovascular and cancer risks. How significant are these?
I have overlapping features of lupus and scleroderma. My rheumatologist calls this mixed connective tissue disease. What does this diagnosis mean?
I have anti-CCP antibodies but no joint symptoms yet. My rheumatologist calls this pre-clinical RA. Should I start treatment before symptoms develop?
I have active RA and I want to get pregnant. Are my RA medications safe during pregnancy?
I have lupus and my complement levels C3 and C4 are low. What does this mean for my disease activity?
I have osteoarthritis in my hips. My rheumatologist and orthopedic surgeon have different opinions on timing for joint replacement. How is this decided?
I was prescribed rituximab for refractory RA. My biologic TNF inhibitor stopped working. How does rituximab work differently?
I have lupus and my rheumatologist monitors my complement and anti-dsDNA levels at each visit. How do these labs relate to lupus flares?
I have severe gout in multiple joints and my uric acid is 11. My rheumatologist wants to get my uric acid below 6. Why is this specific target important?
I have inflammatory arthritis and need a vaccine before starting a biologic. Which vaccines should I get and which are contraindicated on biologics?

I have RA that is well controlled but my recent X-rays show new joint erosions. My rheumatologist says this means inadequate disease control despite feeling well. What does subclinical joint damage mean?
